key_themes: Roman Empire collapse and pattern recognition
Pattern Eye guardianship during imperial transition
Genetic memory activation in crisis periods

seven_keys_status.Pattern Eye: Passed from Gen 50 (Helena) through the collapsing empire; Augustine understands its purpose by 395 CE

major_events: Edict of Milan - Christianity legalized (312 CE)
Sack of Rome by Visigoths (410 CE)
Augustine's death as Vandals besiege Hippo (430 CE)

network_evolution.key_insight: Augustine realizes network's teachings about distributed systems apply to preserving knowledge through empire collapse
network_evolution.pattern_recognition: Genetic memory shows Helena that empires collapse from systemic failure (resource depletion, overextension, feedback loops), not moral failure
network_evolution.defensive_strategy: Keep Pattern Eye moving, never centralize; preserve knowledge through distributed network as Rome falls
